extraction.one_line_summary: HART Tao Gagan won the 2020 Giro d'Italia by 39 seconds over HINDLEY Jai after overturning a time tie on the final day 15.7km Milan ITT
tactical_patterns: GANNA Filippo's fourth stage win reveals Ineos used him strategically throughout race, not just as ITT specialist finisher.
DENNIS Rowan's mountain work paid dividends supporting HART's ITT advantage, showing domestique selection matters for GC ITT finishes.
Mid-race time check at 10km showing HART up 30 seconds allowed tactical confidence adjustment in final 5.7km.

rider_signals.HART Tao Gagan: positive: Started race as domestique, previous best GC was 20th place, yet won—suggests late-race form progression went undetected by rivals
rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: negative: Lost 39 seconds in 15.7km ITT despite being tied on GC shows critical weakness in specialist discipline against polyvalent climbers
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: Four stage wins including both ITTs demonstrates complete domination when Ineos deploys him for stage hunting rather than tempo
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: Moved from fifth to fourth on final stage ITT, revealing stronger chrono ability than GC positioning suggested
